evidence: QueryExecutor.java line 133: @Deprecated on executeDependencyPattern(String,String,String,double,int) overload with Javadoc pointing to two new replacements
Main.java line 219: active call to the deprecated overload — executor.executeDependencyPattern(lemma, deprel, null, minLogDice, limit) — passing null as headPosConstraint
ComparisonResult.java lines 78-80: @Deprecated getEdges() delegates to buildEdges(); no internal callers found
ExplorationResult.java lines 84-86: @Deprecated getEdges() delegates to buildEdges(); no internal callers found

BlackLabQueryExecutor.java lines 246-270: duplicate consecutive Javadoc blocks; first block describes a 'Legacy format (deprecated): %s-style substitution' that no longer exists — the else branch throws IllegalArgumentException

RelationConfig.java lines 193-203: tag= attribute path commented as 'Legacy: IPI_PAN Polish grammar... Keep this path as long as non-English grammars are supported' — intentional, documented backward-compat

strengths: Deprecated overloads carry explicit @Deprecated annotation with Javadoc @deprecated tags pointing to replacement methods
The deprecated shims correctly delegate to new split methods — no functional regression
ComparisonResult and ExplorationResult model classes both renamed the public method to buildEdges() with a clear deprecated getEdges() shim, showing intentional API cleanup
The tag= backward-compat path in RelationConfig is explicitly comment-justified with language-support rationale
incomplete_migration.issue_character: The migration is largely complete and well-marked; the residual issue is a single active call site in Main.java that still invokes the deprecated overload rather than the new split methods, plus two orphaned model shims with no callers.
incomplete_migration.score_rationale: The codebase shows deliberate, annotated migration work: deprecated symbols are clearly marked and the shims correctly delegate. The one concrete failure is Main.java line 219, which calls the deprecated three-argument executeDependencyPattern instead of the new zero-headPos overload, keeping the migration incomplete. The duplicate Javadoc in buildBcqlWithLemmaSubstitution describing a removed code path is a minor cleanup item. Overall this is a 'mostly finished migration with one forgotten call site' pattern — solid enough to score well above 60, but the active deprecated call prevents a score above 80.

issues.summary: Main.java calls deprecated executeDependencyPattern three-argument overload instead of new split methods
evidence: Main.java line 219: executor.executeDependencyPattern(lemma, deprel, null, minLogDice, limit) calls the @Deprecated(String,String,String,double,int) overload
QueryExecutor.java line 133: @deprecated Javadoc says 'Use executeDependencyPattern(String,String,double,int) or executeDependencyPatternWithPos'
issues.suggestion: Replace the deprecated call with executor.executeDependencyPattern(lemma, deprel, minLogDice, limit) — the null headPosConstraint means the two-argument overload is the correct target

issues.summary: Deprecated getEdges() shims in ComparisonResult and ExplorationResult have no remaining callers
evidence: ComparisonResult.java line 79: @Deprecated getEdges() delegates to buildEdges()
ExplorationResult.java line 85: @Deprecated getEdges() delegates to buildEdges()
No calls to getEdges() found in any production source file — the shims have outlived their purpose
issues.suggestion: Remove getEdges() from both model classes; all call sites already use buildEdges(). Verify with 'grep -rn getEdges src/main' before removal.

issues.summary: buildBcqlWithLemmaSubstitution has a duplicate Javadoc block describing a removed legacy %s substitution path
evidence: Lines 246-264: two consecutive /** */ Javadoc blocks appear; the first describes a 'Legacy format (deprecated): %s-style substitution' that no longer exists
The actual else branch at line 269 throws IllegalArgumentException — the legacy path is gone but the first Javadoc still describes it as if it works
issues.suggestion: Remove the first (stale) Javadoc block entirely; the second block correctly describes the current behaviour. The reference to legacy %s support in the interface Javadoc (QueryExecutor.findCollocations) should also be removed.

root_causes: Migration to split dependency-pattern API was completed in the interface and implementation but the CLI call site in Main.java was not updated
likely_symptoms: Compilation warning about deprecated API in Main.java
possible_false_positives: tag= backward-compat in RelationConfig is intentional and should not be treated as incomplete migration
